items.title: CMS: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds for full benefits limited for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS says states must implement a statutory change starting Oct. 1, 2026 that limits federal matching funds (FFP) for full-scope Medicaid and CHIP for people who are not U.S. citizens/nationals unless they fall into specific noncitizen categories in the statute. CMS also notes federal matching funds remain available for emergency Medicaid services and that the change does not affect states’ CHIPRA 214 option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women, nor CHIP Health Services Initiatives.

items.title: CMS SHO #26-001 details who can qualify for full-scope Medicaid/CHIP federal match on/after Oct. 1, 2026 and sets SPA deadlines
items.summary: CMS’ State Health Official letter (SHO #26-001) says that on/after Oct. 1, 2026, full-scope Medicaid/CHIP federal matching funds are limited to lawful permanent residents, Cuban/Haitian entrants, and Compact of Free Association (COFA) migrants (with other exceptions such as emergency Medicaid and the CHIPRA 214 option). CMS instructs states to identify and redetermine potentially affected beneficiaries and says Medicaid SPAs must be submitted by Dec. 31, 2026 (separate CHIP states have different SPA timing, including a Nov. 30, 2026 deadline for some states).

items.title: HUD ends documentation waiver for EHV and Stability Voucher admissions; PHAs must comply within 60 days
items.summary: HUD PIH Notice 2026-05 removes a waiver/alternative requirement that had allowed PHAs to admit Emergency Housing Voucher (EHV) and Stability Voucher (SV) families without first obtaining and verifying Social Security number documentation and eligible noncitizen status documentation. HUD says PHAs must comply within 60 days of publication, including being in compliance with eligible immigration status verification and ensuring SSNs for family members admitted during the waiver period are verified in EIV or otherwise documented.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD: $337 million Public Housing Operating Fund shortfall set-aside; applications due May 5, 2026
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-10 describes Public Housing Operating Fund shortfall funding for FY 2026 and cites a $337 million shortfall set-aside in the Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2026. The notice sets a deadline of 5:00 p.m. ET on May 5, 2026 for both applications and appeals.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: USDA sets FY2026 WIC fruit/vegetable cash-value benefit amounts; April 2026 deadline for added forms of produce
items.summary: USDA WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY 2026 monthly cash-value benefit amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/mostly breastfeeding participants. USDA also notes that state agencies must authorize at least one additional form of fruits and vegetables (frozen, canned, and/or dried) in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: USDA requests public comments on SNAP-Ed reporting system changes; comments due May 20, 2026
items.summary: USDA published a request for comments tied to SNAP-Ed reporting and recordkeeping requirements (including state plan and annual report submissions through N-PEARS). USDA says comments received by May 20, 2026 will be considered, and directs commenters to submit feedback via Reginfo.gov under the 30-day public comment listing.

items.title: IRS: 2026 filing season opened Jan. 26; most EITC/ACTC refunds expected by March 2, 2026
items.summary: The IRS says it opened the 2026 filing season on Jan. 26, 2026 and expects most refunds claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) to be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers using direct deposit and with no other issues. The IRS also says its Where’s My Refund tool will provide projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; Social Security payments start Jan. 2026 and SSI starts Dec. 31, 2025
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA page says Social Security and Supplemental Security Income (SSI) benefits will increase 2.8% in 2026. SSA says the increase begins with benefits payable to nearly 71 million Social Security beneficiaries in January 2026, and increased payments to nearly 7.5 million SSI recipients begin Dec. 31, 2025.

items.title: Federal student aid deadline reminder: 2025–26 FAFSA must be received by June 30, 2026
items.summary: The Department of Education’s 2025–26 FAFSA paper form states that for federal aid, the FAFSA must be received no later than June 30, 2026 (students can still qualify for aid including Pell Grants if they meet other requirements). Federal Student Aid’s partner guidance also emphasizes there are no exceptions: online FAFSA information must be received by June 30, 2026 and paper forms must arrive in time for the processor to receive them by that date.
